title: Whole-Body Bone Scintigraphy Research
subtitle: From annotation to thesis: a 3-year research arc

highlights: Joined as the primary coder for the MoHE-funded bone scan research project
Meticulously annotated 600+ whole-body bone scintigraphy (WBBS) images using IbisPaintX, supervised by nuclear medicine physicians at Dr. Hasan Sadikin General Hospital
Built a 3,000-line Python codebase for confidence scoring, model validation, and BSI quantification
Architected the YOLOv8 hotspot detection and nnU-Net v2 segmentation pipeline
Collaborated directly with doctors at the Department of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Theranostics, Universitas Padjadjaran, including on-site visits to Dr. Hasan Sadikin General Hospital
Extended the annotation dataset to 3,593 WBBS images (anterior + posterior views across 12 skeletal regions)
Participated in developing Telplastina v1, the first deployable diagnostic application for the pipeline
Preprint submitted: 'From Segmentation to Biomarker Quantification' (SSRN)
Elevated the research to Bachelor's Thesis, formalising the full pipeline as an academic contribution
Led development of Telplastina v2, a full-stack WBBS diagnostic app (Vue 3 + Rust + Docker)
Journal paper published: 'Deep Learning-Based Segmentation of Whole-Body Bone Scan Images Using nnU-Netv2' (IJIES-INASS)
Released annotated dataset of 3,593 images publicly on Zenodo for the research community
